_meta.scope: Brand-agnostic. One shared dictionary per PRD Section 9 ('One global regulatory dictionary (brand-agnostic — it's law, not voice)'). Do not duplicate these rules inside brand_configs.json.
_meta.source: Extracted from Half Magic and Tower 28 brand voice docs' Section 4 (Compliance Sensitivities) — the subset that is legal/regulatory, not brand-specific voice preference.

rules.description: Cosmetics cannot claim to heal, treat, cure, or eliminate a diagnosable medical skin condition (e.g. eczema, rosacea, acne). Claiming to treat a named condition crosses into FDA drug-claim territory.
rules.safe_framing: Frame as "safe for" or "compliant with guidelines for" the skin condition — never as treating, curing, or eliminating it.

rules.description: No medical/clinical claims without real substantiation for that specific product.
rules.exception: "Collagen boosting" and similar structure-function claims are permitted only if backed by real substantiating data for that specific product — not by default.

rules.description: "Hypoallergenic" has no official FDA definition. Never use as a bare, unsupported claim.
rules.requirement: Anchor to third-party substantiation (e.g. HRIPT — Human Repeat Insult Patch Test) whenever the word is used.

rules.description: Products with antimicrobial/antibacterial actives (e.g. hypochlorous acid) risk EPA/FDA drug classification if marketed as a sanitizer or disinfectant.
rules.safe_framing: Use cosmetic-appropriate framing instead: soothes, purifies, calms, refreshes, hits refresh on stressed skin.

rules.description: When referencing a third-party seal/certification program (e.g. National Eczema Association Seal of Acceptance), describe compliance with the program's ingredient guidelines only — never imply the organization medically endorses or prescribes the product.
"Dermatologist tested," "allergy tested," and similar testing claims are only usable when backed by verified third-party clinical trial results.
rules.distinction: Different from "dermatologist recommended," which implies a professional endorsement most brands cannot substantiate and should be avoided regardless of testing status.

rules.description: Loose glitter, pressed pigments, and graphic liner products used near the eye area may require usage guidance depending on formulation and regional regulation.
rules.requirement: Include standard eye-area usage guidance by default for eye-adjacent glitter/pigment/liner products unless product-specific documentation says otherwise.

rules.description: Bright/vivid pigments may carry U.S.-specific labeling requirements for eye-area use.
rules.note: Verify per product/SKU and market — not a blanket rule for every colored product.
